overview: Build a deterministic unit-test suite for the existing LiveChat-Bot codebase so each runtime module is covered after refactoring, while preserving current production behavior and API contracts.
goals: Add automated unit tests for every runtime TypeScript module under src (excluding characterization, architecture notes, and declaration files).
Guarantee each module has at least one positive-path assertion and one negative-path assertion.
Keep current behavior stable by running characterization and build/lint gates alongside the new unit-test suite.
Provide a repeatable module-to-test matrix gate that fails when a new module has no corresponding unit tests.
nonGoals: No functional refactor or feature work beyond what is required to enable deterministic tests.
No breaking change to overlay REST/socket contracts or Discord command behavior.
No edits to prisma/migrations unless explicitly requested in a separate task.
No replacement of the existing characterization suite.
successMetrics: 100% of runtime modules in src are mapped to at least one unit test file.
Unit-test suite passes locally and in CI using pnpm test:unit and pnpm test:unit:matrix.
Existing quality gates pnpm lint, pnpm build, and pnpm characterization continue to pass.
Regression defects attributable to refactor drift are caught by unit tests before merge.
openQuestions: Should test completeness be enforced as 100% module-mapping only, or also with a minimum line/branch coverage threshold?
Should command modules that only build Discord metadata require deep behavior tests or smoke-level contract tests?
Should generated Prisma client wrappers be tested with mocks only, or with an optional SQLite integration layer in a separate suite?

stack.framework: Node.js TypeScript backend with Fastify + Discord.js
stack.database: Prisma ORM with SQLite by default
stack.auth: Overlay/socket authentication with pairing codes and ingest credentials from runtime config

routes.name: Overlay Pair Consume
routes.purpose: Validate backward-compatible pairing consume behavior through unit-level route/handler tests.

routes.name: Overlay Config
routes.purpose: Assert returned overlay runtime configuration remains stable.

routes.name: Overlay Transient Media
routes.purpose: Test transient media access and error paths without contract drift.

routes.name: Admin and Ingest Domains
routes.purpose: Verify route registration, auth guards, and handler validation boundaries.

uiNotes: No browser UI is added in this initiative; user-facing verification is via Discord command responses and API payload contracts.
Test naming should mirror domain responsibilities to stay readable during refactor maintenance.
Use stable fixture data and deterministic clocks to avoid flaky outputs in command/message snapshots.

importFormat.description: Not applicable. This PRD does not introduce external data import; inputs are local fixtures and module inventory scans.

rules: Every runtime module must have at least one colocated or mirrored unit test file linked by deterministic naming rules.
Each module test set must include at least one happy path and one negative/error path assertion.
Unit tests must not call real network services, Discord API, or external binaries; those dependencies must be mocked or stubbed.
Characterization baselines remain the behavior safety net and must continue to pass unchanged.
New modules added after this initiative must fail the matrix gate until tests are added.
Test fixtures should avoid relying on wall-clock time or filesystem race conditions.

stories.title: Set up deterministic unit-test foundation
stories.description: As a maintainer, I want a stable unit-test toolchain and scripts so that module-level tests can run consistently on local and CI environments.
acceptanceCriteria: Install required dev dependencies with explicit commands: pnpm add -D vitest @vitest/coverage-v8.
Add package scripts test:unit and test:unit:coverage, plus initial Vitest config tuned for a Node TypeScript backend.
Example: running pnpm test:unit with a sample module test returns exit code 0 and prints at least one passing test.
Negative case: a deliberately failing assertion in a sample test causes pnpm test:unit to exit non-zero.
pnpm lint and pnpm build still pass after test framework setup.

stories.title: Create module inventory and missing-test gate
stories.description: As a maintainer, I want an automated runtime module inventory so that we can enforce one-or-more unit tests for every module.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a deterministic inventory script that scans src runtime modules and excludes characterization, architecture docs, declaration files, and typecheck-only files.
Add script pnpm test:unit:matrix that fails when inventory finds modules with no mapped test files.
Example: when a module has a matching test file, test:unit:matrix reports it as covered.
Negative case: when a new module is added without a test file, test:unit:matrix fails and prints the missing module path.
Matrix output is machine-readable enough for CI logs and developer troubleshooting.

stories.title: Backfill tests for env configuration modules
stories.description: As a maintainer, I want unit tests for env parsing and runtime config modules so that startup behavior remains stable after refactor.
acceptanceCriteria: Cover src/services/env.ts and src/services/env/* modules including configSchema, defaults, parsers, and runtimeConfig behavior.
Assert explicit parsing behavior for required vars, optional vars, defaults, and normalization.
Example: missing optional variable uses configured default and yields the same resulting runtime config value.
Negative case: invalid numeric or enum env value returns the same startup validation error category as current behavior.
All added tests are counted as covered by pnpm test:unit:matrix.

stories.title: Backfill tests for auth and validation services
stories.description: As a maintainer, I want unit tests for auth and request-validation services so that API and socket guardrails do not regress.
acceptanceCriteria: Cover src/services/overlayAuth.ts, src/services/ingestAuth.ts, src/services/pairingCodes.ts, and src/services/validation/requestParsing.ts.
Include tests for accepted credentials, rejected credentials, and parsing error branching.
Example: valid overlay pairing token is accepted and yields expected authenticated context.
Negative case: malformed or expired token path returns unauthorized/validation failure without leaking internal details.
No test performs real external calls; dependencies are mocked.

stories.title: Backfill tests for media service pipeline
stories.description: As a maintainer, I want unit tests for media ingestion, caching, source resolving, and lifecycle orchestration so that transient media behavior stays reliable.
acceptanceCriteria: Cover src/services/media/* modules and related error types, including ingestion, transcode boundaries, cache interactions, and cleanup logic.
Use filesystem/temp-directory mocks to keep tests deterministic and side-effect safe.
Example: valid transient media input stores expected metadata and returns a resolvable transientMediaId.
Negative case: unsupported media format or failed source resolution returns typed media error and triggers cleanup path.
All media modules are listed as covered by pnpm test:unit:matrix.

stories.title: Backfill tests for socket loader lifecycle
stories.description: As a maintainer, I want unit tests for socket authentication, connection state, and event dispatch modules so that overlay socket behavior remains stable.
acceptanceCriteria: Cover src/loaders/socketLoader.ts and src/loaders/socket/* modules, including connection setup, auth checks, and event routing.
Assert emitted event payload shapes and lifecycle transitions using mocked socket interfaces.
Example: authenticated client lifecycle registers handlers and dispatches expected overlay events.
Negative case: unauthenticated client is rejected and no privileged event handlers are attached.
Socket module coverage is recognized by pnpm test:unit:matrix.

stories.title: Backfill tests for REST loader and route domains
stories.description: As a maintainer, I want unit-level route registration and handler tests for REST domains so that protected API contracts remain unchanged.
acceptanceCriteria: Cover src/loaders/RESTLoader.ts, src/loaders/rest/*, and route modules under src/components/overlay, src/components/admin, and src/components/ingest.
Validate route registration and key handler responses with Fastify injection-style tests or equivalent isolated handler tests.
Example: valid POST /overlay/pair/consume returns the expected status and response payload contract.
Negative case: malformed consume payload returns existing validation error status/body shape.
Protected routes assert auth guard behavior for valid and invalid credentials.

stories.title: Backfill tests for Discord loader internals
stories.description: As a maintainer, I want unit tests for Discord command registration and interaction execution internals so that command wiring does not drift.
acceptanceCriteria: Cover src/loaders/DiscordLoader.ts and src/loaders/discord/* modules including metadata mapping, registry building, and execution dispatch.
Mock Discord.js client and interaction objects to avoid real Discord API calls.
Example: known command metadata is registered and dispatched to the expected execution handler.
Negative case: unknown command or execution exception follows existing error-handling path without crashing loader flow.
Module inventory marks all Discord loader internals as covered.

stories.title: Backfill tests for Discord command modules
stories.description: As a maintainer, I want unit tests for each Discord command module so that user-facing command behavior remains consistent.
acceptanceCriteria: Cover all command modules under src/components/discord/* including info/help/alive/meme/overlay and settings commands.
Assert both successful command execution payloads and validation or permission-related failure behavior.
Example: alive command returns expected localized success response structure.
Negative case: invalid command argument (for example bad time value) produces expected validation error response path.
Each command module is mapped as covered in the matrix gate.

stories.title: Backfill tests for message command modules
stories.description: As a maintainer, I want unit tests for message command modules and worker logic so that overlay message flow remains reliable.
acceptanceCriteria: Cover src/components/messages/* and related helper modules used directly by message command flows.
Validate payload construction, scheduling/stop interactions, and translation handling through isolated tests.
Example: talk/send command builds expected worker payload and triggers downstream call once.
Negative case: empty or blocked message input follows refusal/error path with no outbound side effect.
Message modules are fully represented in pnpm test:unit:matrix output.

stories.title: Backfill tests for shared services and adapters
stories.description: As a maintainer, I want unit tests for remaining shared services and adapter modules so that cross-domain utility behavior is protected.
acceptanceCriteria: Cover remaining runtime modules including src/index.ts, src/server.ts, src/services/* non-media/non-auth modules, src/services/social/*, and src/repositories/prisma/*.
Mock process-level and external IO dependencies for deterministic test behavior.
Example: social resolver normalizes a supported URL and returns expected parsed metadata shape.
Negative case: external fetch/runtime dependency failure is converted to expected typed error handling path.
After this story, the matrix report shows no uncovered modules outside explicit exclusions.

stories.title: Enforce gates and document unit-test workflow
stories.description: As a maintainer, I want CI/local workflow documentation and enforced quality gates so that full module-level test coverage persists after future changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Document how to run pnpm test:unit, pnpm test:unit:matrix, pnpm characterization, pnpm lint, and pnpm build for day-to-day and CI use.
Integrate unit-test and matrix commands into repository quality workflow so missing tests block merge.
Example: with all tests present, full gate sequence passes on a clean branch.
Negative case: adding a new module without tests fails matrix gate and prevents green CI.
Final validation run confirms full gate pass and zero uncovered runtime modules.
